upgrade_review.upgrade_delta_one_line: The proposal names a real computation delta, but the scalar-lambda reduction, TU claim, and exhibit do not yet deliver a valid quantitative certificate.

repo_prior_art.cite: none beyond parent pid_rdd_manipulation_bounded/v1 one-cutoff candidate-ratio LP route

published_prior_art.cite: rosenman_rajkumar_gauriot_slonim_2019_discrete_manipulation: manipulated-RD partial-identification procedure, no verified laminar TU certificate
gerard_rokkanen_rothe_2020_rdbounds: one-cutoff sharp manipulated-RD bounds; bertanha_2020_many_thresholds: many-threshold point-ID RD

repo_prior_art.cite: none beyond parent lacking a dual certificate

published_prior_art.cite: chernozhukov_lee_rosen_2013_intersection_bounds: indexed/intersection-bound inference, not a manipulated-RD dual certificate

novelty_flags.label: upgrade
novelty_flags.one_line: Computation rubric is only partially delivered: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship because the claimed strongly polynomial certificate has no valid algorithmic bound or worked certificate yet.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1 / Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The constructive objects are the endpoint algorithm and dual certificate, but Exhibit 9.1 computes neither endpoint values nor dual potentials from primitives.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The Rosenman et al. comparator is cited without a section, equation, page, or quoted claim tying it to the specific missing laminar-flow/TU certificate.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The retained-W constraints contain arbitrary coefficients pi^-_j(w), so the displayed constraint matrix is not a node-arc incidence matrix or TU without an additional integral/splitting construction.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: A single scalar lambda does not recover the support function of a vector of ratios for general u in R_+^J; it only fits a one-ratio or coordinate-endpoint problem.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The proposed dual omits dual variables for capacity/upper-feasibility/envelope constraints and does not specify the fractional-program dual whose value equals h.

soundness_flags.label: Exhibit 9.1
soundness_flags.one_line: The worked J=2 instance is infeasible: for j=1 the available right-side mass is 0.50, but the exhibit sets m_1=0.80 and demands cell retentions 0.48 and 0.32.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The proof route names parametric search but gives no breakpoint bound, parametric-flow theorem, or algorithmic reduction sufficient for the strongly polynomial claim.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the scalar-lambda support-function claim with either coordinate endpoint problems or a valid multi-ratio/vector-multiplier formulation.
Repair Exhibit 9.1 with feasible annulus masses, then compute the actual flow optimum, endpoint value, active cap, and dual potentials.
Add an explicit algorithm statement with input size, network construction, parametric-search theorem, and complexity bound.
Add location-level anchors for Rosenman et al. and the many-threshold RD comparators, and update the Rosenman arXiv title if using the current version.

recommended_next_step: Revise the computation kernel before Stage 0: fix the multi-ratio/TU algebra and replace the infeasible exhibit with a computed certificate.
